this felt.<|11.00|> <|11.00|> Don commented that it was incredible that something we couldn't even see<|17.00|> <|17.00|> had turned our habits and our world upside down.<|22.00|> <|22.00|> We were suddenly distancing, disinfecting, and dialing in for work.<|28.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Though, as we kept talking about it, we realized that as people of faith, we <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> In this week after Easter, after the Lentiest Lent ever, we are encountering stories of<|7.84|> <|7.84|> disciples who struggle to figure out how to readers and believers, have the insight to know what the disciples could not grasp at that moment,<|9.38|> <|9.86|> that even if it is not visible, it can still be true.<|15.54|> <|16.58|> God is <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Peter and the disciple Jesus loved did not take Mary Magdalene at her word.<|6.20|> <|6.20|> They ran to the tomb to see for themselves that it was empty.<|11.20|> <|11.20|> After Mary Magdalene has a conversation with the risen Christ, she went and announced to the disciples, "I have seen the Lord."<|20.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> This continues as a pattern for which Thomas is rather unfairly singled out and maligned.<|8.98|> <|10.30|> The evening of the first day, most of the disciples had locked themselves in a house out of fear.<|18.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Jesus appeared to them, and as John tells us, he showed them his hands and his side.<|7.38|> <|8.36|> Then the disciples rejoiced when they saw the Lord.<|12.32|> <|13.22|> Then they rejoiced.<|14.52|> <|15.16|> After they had seen, could they believe?<|19.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We don't know why Thomas wasn't there, but Scripture tells us that he was not.<|5.60|> <|6.50|> The disciples who were present told Thomas of their encounter with Jesus,<|11.34|> <|12.00|> and like them, he was skeptical.<|14.22|> <|15.16|> How could this be?<|16.14|> <|17.04|> He needed to experience Jesus for himself.<|20.94|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Biblical scholars point to this pattern throughout the Gospel of John.<|5.38|> <|5.38|> From the beginning of this Gospel, one person encounters Jesus.<|11.04|> <|11.04|> Then they share their experience with the next person who may express some reluctance.<|17.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Then that person experiences disciples, as does Thomas, as do we.<|9.18|> <|10.86|> I remember a wise older woman at church telling me when our children were young<|16.78|> <|16.78|> that God has no grandchildren, only children.<|22.28|> <|22.94|> My faith could not ensure my children's.<|27.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> they would have to experience God and claim their own belief.<|5.06|> and trust when he has experienced the risen Christ,<|24.88|> <|24.88|> my Lord and my God.<|27.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And Jesus, in his grace, makes a way for us and And Jesus came and stood among them and said, peace be with you. Jesus appeared<|11.78|> <|11.78|> mysteriously where they were, not in the temple, not in the synagogue, in their house, where they were.<|23.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Jesus came to them through locked doors in their isolation, in their confusion, in their fear.<|9.82|> <|11.02|> We know Easter Sunday happened, at least that's what the calendar told us,<|15.68|> <|16.00|> and it was unlike any Easter Sunday we have ever experienced.<|20.88|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But it is still a bit hard to believe it is Easter, isn't it?<|6.16|> outside the tomb.<|10.28|> <|11.64|> Jesus met most of the disciples in one locked house<|15.38|> <|15.38|> and the disciples joined by Thomas in another locked house.<|20.74|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Jesus meets us and those we love in our own homes, in a hospital room, in a nursing home, in hospice care.<|14.02|> <|14.94|> There is no level of
